Macros for calculating array lengths and member sizes in C.

# Purpose
This C header file defines three macros to assist with array and structure size calculations. The `ARRAY_LENGTH` macro calculates the number of elements in an array by dividing the total size of the array by the size of its first element. The `STATIC_ARRAY_LENGTH` macro computes the length of a literal array in bytes, which is useful in contexts that require compile-time constants. The `MEMBER_SIZE` macro determines the size of a specific member within a structure by using a null pointer cast to the structure type and accessing the member. These macros are useful for developers who need to perform size calculations in a type-safe and efficient manner.
